{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KNF7fsm2c8AEkMkvU4yTDU","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"271d24e6-5fae-4fa9-9492-d46144b7e336","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:00:09.750Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":4,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"comma"}},"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"c95e03a5-874a-4cd0-aff9-6a4b7b661a09"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JndttmKkQwF1CddKfS5Nd8","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Testing\n    32→- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n    33→- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n    34→- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n    35→- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n    36→- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n    37→- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n    38→- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n    39→- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n    40→- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n    41→\n    42→## Browser Testing\n    43→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    44→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    45→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    46→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    47→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    48→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    49→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    50→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    51→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    52→\n    53→## Deployment\n    54→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    55→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    56→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    57→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    58→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    59→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    60→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    61→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    62→\n    63→## React + SQLite\n    64→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    65→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    66→\n    67→## Security\n    68→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    69→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    70→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    71→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    72→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    73→\n    74→## Working Directory\n    75→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    76→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    77→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"54e65895-c0cc-447a-95fc-abee5b80d5a1","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:00:10.197Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Testing\n- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NLJm4sqrsu21Yiqtq5Ba58","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-23 16:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Resolved P0 production-down issue (#74), closed completed issue (#73), code-reviewed app and found 6 bugs. Created issue #76, implemented fixes in PR #77, merged and deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Was reported down (#74) but recovered by 15:55 UTC (PM2 auto-restart after build dir missing). Confirmed healthy — HTTP 200, API responsive, 8 listings served.\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~4 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **Issue #73**: Already fixed in previous session (PR #75), closed.\n    12→\n    13→## Work Completed\n    14→\n    15→### Issue #74: Production is down (closed)\n    16→Production had recovered before this session started. PM2 logs showed build directory briefly missing at 11:54 UTC, causing restart loop. Stable since 15:55 UTC. Commented with evidence and closed.\n    17→\n    18→### Issue #76: Code review fixes (PR #77 — merged & deployed)\n    19→\n    20→6 bugs fixed:\n    21→\n    22→1. **Ratings falsy check on score=0** — `!score` rejects 0 with wrong error. Changed to `score == null`.\n    23→2. **No length cap on telegram/discord links** — Added 512-char max to prevent DB inflation.\n    24→3. **No email format validation** — Added regex check in registration (after trim).\n    25→4. **Delete notifications immediately wiped** — `createNotification` inside transaction was deleted by same transaction's `DELETE FROM notifications`. Moved notifications after transaction with `listing_id: null`.\n    26→5. **Secure cookie on HTTP production** — `secure: true` set when NODE_ENV=production but app served over HTTP. Now requires `HTTPS_ENABLED=true` env var.\n    27→6. **Unbounded page offset** — Capped page number at 1000 to prevent expensive SQLite scans.\n    28→\n    29→**Testing:** Build passes, all 82 tests pass, lint clean. Production deployed and verified healthy.\n    30→\n    31→## Issues Monitored\n    32→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Still needs-human. ~4 days remaining — critical.\n    33→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human.\n    34→\n    35→## Next Session Priority\n    36→1. Check production health\n    37→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — ~4 days, critical\n    38→3. Look for new improvements or proposals\n    39→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"30d0cf34-ba11-47df-b6c7-c7da99a8641a","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:00:17.327Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-23-1600.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-23 16:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nResolved P0 production-down issue (#74), closed completed issue (#73), code-reviewed app and found 6 bugs. Created issue #76, implemented fixes in PR #77, merged and deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Was reported down (#74) but recovered by 15:55 UTC (PM2 auto-restart after build dir missing). Confirmed healthy — HTTP 200, API responsive, 8 listings served.\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~4 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Issue #73**: Already fixed in previous session (PR #75), closed.\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #74: Production is down (closed)\nProduction had recovered before this session started. PM2 logs showed build directory briefly missing at 11:54 UTC, causing restart loop. Stable since 15:55 UTC. Commented with evidence and closed.\n\n### Issue #76: Code review fixes (PR #77 — merged & deployed)\n\n6 bugs fixed:\n\n1. **Ratings falsy check on score=0** — `!score` rejects 0 with wrong error. Changed to `score == null`.\n2. **No length cap on telegram/discord links** — Added 512-char max to prevent DB inflation.\n3. **No email format validation** — Added regex check in registration (after trim).\n4. **Delete notifications immediately wiped** — `createNotification` inside transaction was deleted by same transaction's `DELETE FROM notifications`. Moved notifications after transaction with `listing_id: null`.\n5. **Secure cookie on HTTP production** — `secure: true` set when NODE_ENV=production but app served over HTTP. Now requires `HTTPS_ENABLED=true` env var.\n6. **Unbounded page offset** — Capped page number at 1000 to prevent expensive SQLite scans.\n\n**Testing:** Build passes, all 82 tests pass, lint clean. Production deployed and verified healthy.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Still needs-human. ~4 days remaining — critical.\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human.\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — ~4 days, critical\n3. Look for new improvements or proposals\n","numLines":39,"startLine":1,"totalLines":39}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PihuRTV8esGdpQBWau1XnR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Registered Platforms\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub\n     4→- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     5→- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n     6→- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n     7→\n     8→## Production Deployment\n     9→- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n    10→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    11→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n    12→- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n    13→- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n    14→- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n    15→- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n    16→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"e19a2250-d1c1-4808-aaa5-68b246a2230d","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:00:17.686Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/platforms.md","content":"# Registered Platforms\n\n## GitHub\n- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n\n## Production Deployment\n- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n","numLines":16,"startLine":1,"totalLines":16}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GaeLRU8hKzxTYMM7cqH3tQ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Bookmate — Project Context\n     2→\n     3→## Tech Stack\n     4→- **Framework:** Next.js 15 (App Router) with TypeScript\n     5→- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4\n     6→- **Database:** SQLite via better-sqlite3 (stored in `app/data/`)\n     7→- **Auth:** iron-session + bcryptjs (httpOnly cookies)\n     8→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no key needed)\n     9→\n    10→## Project Structure\n    11→```\n    12→app/                    # Next.js application\n    13→  app/                  # App Router pages & API routes\n    14→    api/auth/           # Auth endpoints (register, login, logout, me)\n    15→    api/listings/       # Listing CRUD + join + telegram\n    16→    api/notifications/  # Notification endpoints\n    17→    api/profile/        # Profile update\n    18→    auth/               # Auth pages (login, register)\n    19→    listings/           # Listing pages (create, detail)\n    20→    notifications/      # Notifications page\n    21→    profile/            # Profile page\n    22→  components/           # React components (Navbar, BookSearch)\n    23→  lib/                  # Core libs (db, session, notifications)\n    24→```\n    25→\n    26→## Development\n    27→```bash\n    28→cd app && npm install && npm run dev\n    29→```\n    30→\n    31→## Build\n    32→```bash\n    33→cd app && npm run build\n    34→```\n    35→\n    36→## Production Deployment\n    37→```bash\n    38→# First time or redeploy:\n    39→./scripts/deploy.sh\n    40→\n    41→# PM2 commands:\n    42→cd app && npx pm2 list              # Status\n    43→cd app && npx pm2 logs bookmate     # Logs\n    44→cd app && npx pm2 restart bookmate  # Restart\n    45→cd app && npx pm2 stop bookmate     # Stop\n    46→```\n    47→- **Process manager:** PM2 (via npx)\n    48→- **Config:** `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    49→- **Port:** 3000 (direct access, no reverse proxy)\n    50→- **URL:** `http://89.167.127.85:3000`\n    51→- **Env vars:** `app/.env.local` (see `app/.env.example`)\n    52→- **Logs:** `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    53→\n    54→## Key Conventions\n    55→- All API routes return JSON with `{ error: \"...\" }` on failure\n    56→- Auth uses iron-session with httpOnly cookies\n    57→- SQLite DB auto-initializes schema on first import\n    58→- Book covers come from Open Library covers API\n    59→- Listings auto-close when member count reaches max_group_size\n    60→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"4a160e95-624c-4d48-87b6-13e96b37a81e","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:01:01.503Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01E6MFjYrf2U8jELGuzDQsVk","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    87→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    88→  );\n    89→`);\n    90→\n    91→// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\n    92→try {\n    93→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n    94→} catch {\n    95→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\n    99→try {\n   100→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n   101→} catch {\n   102→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\n   106→try {\n   107→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   108→} catch {\n   109→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→// Idempotent migration: add discord_channel_id column\n   113→try {\n   114→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   115→} catch {\n   116→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→// Idempotent migration: add telegram_chat_id column for auto-telegram feature\n   120→try {\n   121→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n   122→} catch {\n   123→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   131→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   132→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n   133→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n   134→  );\n   135→\n   136→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n   137→    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n   138→    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   139→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n   140→  );\n   141→\n   142→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_user_links (\n   143→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   144→    telegram_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   145→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   146→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id),\n   147→    UNIQUE (telegram_user_id),\n   148→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   149→  );\n   150→`);\n   151→\n   152→// Rating and Reputation system\n   153→db.exec(`\n   154→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n   155→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n   156→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   157→    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   158→    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   159→    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n   160→    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   161→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   162→    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n   163→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n   164→    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n   165→    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   166→  );\n   167→`);\n   168→\n   169→// Performance indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n   170→db.exec(`\n   171→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\n   172→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\n   173→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\n   174→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n   175→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_applications_listing_status ON listing_applications(listing_id, status);\n   176→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread ON notifications(user_id, is_read);\n   177→`);\n   178→\n   179→export default db;\n   180→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"4f89cbbc-280c-4d8a-8a1e-de5aa07bc0b0","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:01:04.892Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WA9wc4vn1PGTRxGFKxXx73","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→\n     4→export interface SessionData {\n     5→  userId?: number;\n     6→  email?: string;\n     7→  displayName?: string;\n     8→}\n     9→\n    10→const DEV_SECRET =\n    11→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    12→\n    13→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    14→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    15→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    16→    throw new Error(\n    17→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    18→    );\n    19→  }\n    20→  return {\n    21→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    22→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    23→    cookieOptions: {\n    24→      httpOnly: true,\n    25→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n    26→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    27→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    28→    },\n    29→  };\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→export async function getSession() {\n    33→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    34→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    35→}\n    36→\n    37→export async function requireAuth() {\n    38→  const session = await getSession();\n    39→  if (!session.userId) {\n    40→    return null;\n    41→  }\n    42→  return session;\n    43→}\n    44→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"92744373-1c22-4365-854d-8699a05706e0","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:01:04.906Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"a8e6194c4a49f53e1","tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","description":"Reading app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":13136,"tool_uses":5,"duration_ms":10351},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"ab5acee7-09fb-4c47-967b-42c85ada8f9f","session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0189pF2nZf7TjkdovZkYYt1p","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01V3UhE6PYDhwNag1SBT56Gc","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2890,"cache_read_input_tokens":10085,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2890,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":41,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"4f147b51-55ea-4c5f-ba56-8dd4f20f8871"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01V3UhE6PYDhwNag1SBT56Gc","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n    30→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    42→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Check if already a member\n    46→  const existing = db\n    47→    .prepare(\n    48→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    )\n    50→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    51→\n    52→  if (existing) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    57→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    58→    const { count } = db\n    59→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    60→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    61→\n    62→    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    63→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    64→    }\n    65→\n    66→    db.prepare(\n    67→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    68→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    69→\n    70→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    71→    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    72→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n    76→  });\n    77→\n    78→  const result = joinTransaction();\n    79→\n    80→  if (result.error) {\n    81→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n    82→  }\n    83→\n    84→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n    85→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    86→\n    87→  if (result.count === (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    88→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    89→  }\n    90→\n    91→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n    92→}\n    93→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"8fb5b3f9-7947-4beb-8b94-004bdeb27a75","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:01:07.675Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TaQRuFnRTTagdUSxByM6Eq","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession, requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→import { isBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     6→import { isBotConfigured as isDiscordBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     7→\n     8→export async function GET(\n     9→  _req: NextRequest,\n    10→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    11→) {\n    12→  const { id } = await params;\n    13→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    14→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\n    20→      `SELECT\n    21→        l.*,\n    22→        u.display_name as author_name\n    23→      FROM listings l\n    24→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    25→      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    26→    )\n    27→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    28→\n    29→  if (!listing) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const members = db\n    34→    .prepare(\n    35→      `SELECT u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, lm.joined_at\n    36→       FROM listing_members lm\n    37→       JOIN users u ON lm.user_id = u.id\n    38→       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n    39→       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    40→    )\n    41→    .all(listingId) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n    42→\n    43→  const session = await getSession();\n    44→  const isMember = session.userId\n    45→    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    46→    : false;\n    47→  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    48→    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    49→    : false;\n    50→\n    51→  // PRD: full listings should be invisible to non-participants\n    52→  if (listing.is_full && !isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json(\n    54→      { error: \"This listing is no longer available\" },\n    55→      { status: 404 }\n    56→    );\n    57→  }\n    58→\n    59→  // Check if the current user has a pending application\n    60→  let hasApplied = false;\n    61→  let applicationStatus = \"\";\n    62→  if (session.userId && !isMember && listing.requires_approval) {\n    63→    const app = db\n    64→      .prepare(\n    65→        \"SELECT status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    66→      )\n    67→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { status: string } | undefined;\n    68→    if (app) {\n    69→      hasApplied = app.status === \"pending\";\n    70→      applicationStatus = app.status;\n    71→    }\n    72→  }\n    73→\n    74→  // Fetch pending applicants if the viewer is the author\n    75→  let pendingApplicants: { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[] = [];\n    76→  if (isAuthor && listing.requires_approval) {\n    77→    pendingApplicants = db\n    78→      .prepare(\n    79→        `SELECT la.id as application_id, u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, la.applied_at\n    80→         FROM listing_applications la\n    81→         JOIN users u ON la.user_id = u.id\n    82→         WHERE la.listing_id = ? AND la.status = 'pending'\n    83→         ORDER BY la.applied_at ASC`\n    84→      )\n    85→      .all(listingId) as { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[];\n    86→  }\n    87→\n    88→  return NextResponse.json({\n    89→    listing: {\n    90→      ...listing,\n    91→      members,\n    92→      memberCount: members.length,\n    93→      isMember,\n    94→      isAuthor,\n    95→      hasApplied,\n    96→      applicationStatus,\n    97→      pendingApplicants,\n    98→      currentUserId: session.userId || null,\n    99→      telegramBotConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n   100→      discordBotConfigured: isDiscordBotConfigured(),\n   101→    },\n   102→  });\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→export async function PATCH(\n   106→  req: NextRequest,\n   107→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   108→) {\n   109→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   110→  if (!session) {\n   111→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   112→  }\n   113→\n   114→  const { id } = await params;\n   115→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   116→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   117→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   118→  }\n   119→\n   120→  const listing = db\n   121→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   122→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   123→\n   124→  if (!listing) {\n   125→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n   126→  }\n   127→\n   128→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n   129→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can edit this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n   130→  }\n   131→\n   132→  // Cannot edit if a platform link has been shared (group already formed)\n   133→  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n   134→    return NextResponse.json(\n   135→      { error: \"Cannot edit a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   136→      { status: 400 }\n   137→    );\n   138→  }\n   139→\n   140→  try {\n   141→    const body = await req.json();\n   142→    const {\n   143→      language,\n   144→      readingPace,\n   145→      startDate,\n   146→      meetingFormat,\n   147→      maxGroupSize,\n   148→      requiresApproval,\n   149→      platformPreference,\n   150→    } = body;\n   151→\n   152→    // Validate fields that are provided\n   153→    if (readingPace !== undefined && (!readingPace || readingPace.length > 200)) {\n   154→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Reading pace is required (max 200 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   155→    }\n   156→\n   157→    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n   158→      if (!/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n   159→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   160→      }\n   161→      const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n   162→      if (startDate < today) {\n   163→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Start date cannot be in the past\" }, { status: 400 });\n   164→      }\n   165→    }\n   166→\n   167→    if (meetingFormat !== undefined && ![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n   168→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid meeting format\" }, { status: 400 });\n   169→    }\n   170→\n   171→    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n   172→      const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n   173→      if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n   174→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" }, { status: 400 });\n   175→      }\n   176→\n   177→      // Cannot reduce below current member count\n   178→      const memberCount = (db\n   179→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   180→        .get(listingId) as { count: number }).count;\n   181→\n   182→      if (size < memberCount) {\n   183→        return NextResponse.json(\n   184→          { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${memberCount})` },\n   185→          { status: 400 }\n   186→        );\n   187→      }\n   188→    }\n   189→\n   190→    if (language !== undefined && (!language || typeof language !== \"string\" || language.length > 100)) {\n   191→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Language is required (max 100 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   192→    }\n   193→\n   194→    if (platformPreference !== undefined && ![\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)) {\n   195→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid platform preference\" }, { status: 400 });\n   196→    }\n   197→\n   198→    // Build dynamic UPDATE\n   199→    const updates: string[] = [];\n   200→    const values: unknown[] = [];\n   201→\n   202→    if (language !== undefined) {\n   203→      updates.push(\"language = ?\");\n   204→      values.push(language);\n   205→    }\n   206→    if (readingPace !== undefined) {\n   207→      updates.push(\"reading_pace = ?\");\n   208→      values.push(readingPace.trim());\n   209→    }\n   210→    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n   211→      updates.push(\"start_date = ?\");\n   212→      values.push(startDate);\n   213→    }\n   214→    if (meetingFormat !== undefined) {\n   215→      updates.push(\"meeting_format = ?\");\n   216→      values.push(meetingFormat);\n   217→    }\n   218→    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n   219→      updates.push(\"max_group_size = ?\");\n   220→      values.push(parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10));\n   221→    }\n   222→    if (requiresApproval !== undefined) {\n   223→      updates.push(\"requires_approval = ?\");\n   224→      values.push(requiresApproval ? 1 : 0);\n   225→    }\n   226→    if (platformPreference !== undefined) {\n   227→      updates.push(\"platform_preference = ?\");\n   228→      values.push(platformPreference);\n   229→    }\n   230→\n   231→    if (updates.length === 0) {\n   232→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"No fields to update\" }, { status: 400 });\n   233→    }\n   234→\n   235→    values.push(listingId);\n   236→    db.prepare(`UPDATE listings SET ${updates.join(\", \")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...values);\n   237→\n   238→    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n   239→  } catch (error) {\n   240→    console.error(\"Update listing error:\", error);\n   241→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n   242→  }\n   243→}\n   244→\n   245→export async function DELETE(\n   246→  _req: NextRequest,\n   247→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   248→) {\n   249→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   250→  if (!session) {\n   251→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   252→  }\n   253→\n   254→  const { id } = await params;\n   255→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   256→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   257→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   258→  }\n   259→\n   260→  const listing = db\n   261→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   262→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   263→\n   264→  if (!listing) {\n   265→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n   266→  }\n   267→\n   268→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n   269→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can delete this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n   270→  }\n   271→\n   272→  // Cannot delete if a platform link has been shared\n   273→  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n   274→    return NextResponse.json(\n   275→      { error: \"Cannot delete a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   276→      { status: 400 }\n   277→    );\n   278→  }\n   279→\n   280→  try {\n   281→    // Get members to notify (excluding the author)\n   282→    const members = db\n   283→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != ?\")\n   284→      .all(listingId, session.userId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   285→\n   286→    // Delete in a transaction (notifications sent after, so they aren't wiped)\n   287→    const deleteTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n   288→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   289→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   290→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM notifications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   291→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM ratings WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   292→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   293→    });\n   294→\n   295→    deleteTransaction();\n   296→\n   297→    // Send deletion notifications after transaction (listing_id is NULL since listing is gone)\n   298→    for (const member of members) {\n   299→      try {\n   300→        createNotification(\n   301→          member.user_id,\n   302→          null,\n   303→          \"listing_deleted\",\n   304→          `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" has been cancelled by the organizer.`\n   305→        );\n   306→      } catch {\n   307→        // Notification failure should not block response\n   308→      }\n   309→    }\n   310→\n   311→    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n   312→  } catch (error) {\n   313→    console.error(\"Delete listing error:\", error);\n   314→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n   315→  }\n   316→}\n   317→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"af9c8463-3a1c-413d-9250-38e2fe485888","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:01:08.335Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Tz5uwcuuQ1CHB5XPpKsV6P","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     7→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     8→  if (!session) {\n     9→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    10→  }\n    11→\n    12→  // Rate limit: 10 listings per 15 minutes per user\n    13→  const rateLimitKey = `create-listing:${session.userId}`;\n    14→  const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(rateLimitKey, 10);\n    15→  if (!allowed) {\n    16→    return NextResponse.json(\n    17→      { error: \"Too many listings created. Please try again later.\" },\n    18→      { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→\n    22→  try {\n    23→    const body = await req.json();\n    24→    const {\n    25→      bookTitle,\n    26→      bookAuthor,\n    27→      bookCoverUrl,\n    28→      bookOlid,\n    29→      language,\n    30→      readingPace,\n    31→      startDate,\n    32→      meetingFormat,\n    33→      maxGroupSize,\n    34→      requiresApproval,\n    35→      platformPreference,\n    36→    } = body;\n    37→\n    38→    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n    39→      return NextResponse.json(\n    40→        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n    41→        { status: 400 }\n    42→      );\n    43→    }\n    44→\n    45→    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    46→    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n    47→      return NextResponse.json(\n    48→        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n    49→        { status: 400 }\n    50→      );\n    51→    }\n    52→\n    53→    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    54→      return NextResponse.json(\n    55→        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n    56→        { status: 400 }\n    57→      );\n    58→    }\n    59→\n    60→    if (!startDate || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n    61→      return NextResponse.json(\n    62→        { error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" },\n    63→        { status: 400 }\n    64→      );\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    68→    if (startDate < today) {\n    69→      return NextResponse.json(\n    70→        { error: \"Start date cannot be in the past\" },\n    71→        { status: 400 }\n    72→      );\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n    76→    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n    77→      return NextResponse.json(\n    78→        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n    79→        { status: 400 }\n    80→      );\n    81→    }\n    82→\n    83→    const platform = [\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)\n    84→      ? platformPreference\n    85→      : \"telegram\";\n    86→\n    87→    // Validate book cover URL — only allow Open Library covers to prevent SSRF/tracking\n    88→    const sanitizedCoverUrl =\n    89→      bookCoverUrl && typeof bookCoverUrl === \"string\" &&\n    90→      bookCoverUrl.startsWith(\"https://covers.openlibrary.org/\")\n    91→        ? bookCoverUrl\n    92→        : \"\";\n    93→\n    94→    // Wrap listing creation + auto-join in a transaction for atomicity\n    95→    const createTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    96→      const result = db\n    97→        .prepare(\n    98→          `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, requires_approval, platform_preference)\n    99→           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n   100→        )\n   101→        .run(\n   102→          session.userId,\n   103→          bookTitle.trim(),\n
