{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ExtMiHK6Yigxi8XnAbiQ1w","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→\n     6→interface Notification {\n     7→  id: number;\n     8→  listing_id: number;\n     9→  type: string;\n    10→  message: string;\n    11→  is_read: number;\n    12→  created_at: string;\n    13→  book_title: string;\n    14→}\n    15→\n    16→export default function NotificationsPage() {\n    17→  const [notifications, setNotifications] = useState<Notification[]>([]);\n    18→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    19→\n    20→  useEffect(() => {\n    21→    fetch(\"/api/notifications\")\n    22→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    23→      .then((data) => {\n    24→        setNotifications(data.notifications || []);\n    25→        setLoading(false);\n    26→        // Mark all as read\n    27→        fetch(\"/api/notifications\", {\n    28→          method: \"PATCH\",\n    29→          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    30→          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n    31→        });\n    32→      });\n    33→  }, []);\n    34→\n    35→  if (loading) {\n    36→    return (\n    37→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}>\n    38→        <p style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>Loading...</p>\n    39→      </div>\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  return (\n    44→    <div className=\"max-w-2xl mx-auto\">\n    45→      <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-3xl mb-4 sm:mb-6\">Notifications</h1>\n    46→\n    47→      {notifications.length === 0 ? (\n    48→        <div className=\"text-center py-12\">\n    49→          <p style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>\n    50→            No notifications yet\n    51→          </p>\n    52→        </div>\n    53→      ) : (\n    54→        <div className=\"space-y-2\">\n    55→          {notifications.map((n) => (\n    56→            <Link\n    57→              key={n.id}\n    58→              href={n.listing_id ? `/listings/${n.listing_id}` : \"#\"}\n    59→              className=\"card block hover:shadow-sm transition-shadow\"\n    60→              style={{\n    61→                borderLeftWidth: \"3px\",\n    62→                borderLeftColor: n.is_read\n    63→                  ? \"var(--color-border)\"\n    64→                  : \"var(--color-accent)\",\n    65→              }}\n    66→              aria-label={`${!n.is_read ? \"Unread: \" : \"\"}${n.message}`}\n    67→            >\n    68→              {!n.is_read && (\n    69→                <span className=\"sr-only\">Unread</span>\n    70→              )}\n    71→              <p\n    72→                className=\"text-sm\"\n    73→                style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    74→              >\n    75→                {n.message}\n    76→              </p>\n    77→              <p\n    78→                className=\"text-xs mt-1\"\n    79→                style={{\n    80→                  color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n    81→                  fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    82→                }}\n    83→              >\n    84→                {new Date(n.created_at).toLocaleDateString(\"en-US\", {\n    85→                  month: \"short\",\n    86→                  day: \"numeric\",\n    87→                  hour: \"numeric\",\n    88→                  minute: \"2-digit\",\n    89→                })}\n    90→              </p>\n    91→            </Link>\n    92→          ))}\n    93→        </div>\n    94→      )}\n    95→    </div>\n    96→  );\n    97→}\n    98→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"d6d3fdca-a6a9-42f4-8c4b-4f8e0be657a0","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:01:49.582Z"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01U9PR46SASuykmHaNCR8kGu","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useParams, useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import Image from \"next/image\";\n     6→\n     7→export default function EditListingPage() {\n     8→  const { id } = useParams();\n     9→  const router = useRouter();\n    10→  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n    11→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    12→  const [fetching, setFetching] = useState(true);\n    13→\n    14→  const [bookTitle, setBookTitle] = useState(\"\");\n    15→  const [bookAuthor, setBookAuthor] = useState(\"\");\n    16→  const [bookCoverUrl, setBookCoverUrl] = useState(\"\");\n    17→  const [language, setLanguage] = useState(\"English\");\n    18→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    19→  const [startDate, setStartDate] = useState(\"\");\n    20→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"text\");\n    21→  const [maxGroupSize, setMaxGroupSize] = useState(\"4\");\n    22→  const [requiresApproval, setRequiresApproval] = useState(false);\n    23→  const [platformPreference, setPlatformPreference] = useState(\"telegram\");\n    24→  const [memberCount, setMemberCount] = useState(1);\n    25→\n    26→  useEffect(() => {\n    27→    async function fetchListing() {\n    28→      try {\n    29→        const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}`);\n    30→        const data = await res.json();\n    31→        if (res.ok && data.listing) {\n    32→          const l = data.listing;\n    33→          if (!l.isAuthor) {\n    34→            router.push(`/listings/${id}`);\n    35→            return;\n    36→          }\n    37→          // Cannot edit if platform link shared\n    38→          if (l.telegram_link || l.discord_link) {\n    39→            router.push(`/listings/${id}`);\n    40→            return;\n    41→          }\n    42→          setBookTitle(l.book_title);\n    43→          setBookAuthor(l.book_author);\n    44→          setBookCoverUrl(l.book_cover_url || \"\");\n    45→          setLanguage(l.language || \"English\");\n    46→          setReadingPace(l.reading_pace || \"\");\n    47→          setStartDate(l.start_date || \"\");\n    48→          setMeetingFormat(l.meeting_format || \"text\");\n    49→          setMaxGroupSize(String(l.max_group_size || 4));\n    50→          setRequiresApproval(!!l.requires_approval);\n    51→          setPlatformPreference(l.platform_preference || \"telegram\");\n    52→          setMemberCount(l.memberCount || 1);\n    53→        } else if (res.status === 401) {\n    54→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    55→        } else {\n    56→          setError(\"Listing not found\");\n    57→        }\n    58→      } catch {\n    59→        setError(\"Failed to load listing\");\n    60→      } finally {\n    61→        setFetching(false);\n    62→      }\n    63→    }\n    64→    fetchListing();\n    65→  }, [id, router]);\n    66→\n    67→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    68→    e.preventDefault();\n    69→    setError(\"\");\n    70→    setLoading(true);\n    71→\n    72→    try {\n    73→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}`, {\n    74→        method: \"PATCH\",\n    75→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    76→        body: JSON.stringify({\n    77→          language,\n    78→          readingPace,\n    79→          startDate,\n    80→          meetingFormat,\n    81→          maxGroupSize,\n    82→          requiresApproval,\n    83→          platformPreference,\n    84→        }),\n    85→      });\n    86→\n    87→      const data = await res.json();\n    88→\n    89→      if (!res.ok) {\n    90→        if (res.status === 401) {\n    91→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    92→          return;\n    93→        }\n    94→        setError(data.error || \"Failed to update listing\");\n    95→        return;\n    96→      }\n    97→\n    98→      router.push(`/listings/${id}`);\n    99→    } catch {\n   100→      setError(\"Something went wrong. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01D4z8pV48UsvWASsBBgvgpv","type":"tool_result","content":"    54→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    55→  listingId: number,\n    56→  joinerName: string\n    57→) {\n    58→  const listing = db\n    59→    .prepare(\n    60→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    61→    )\n    62→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    63→\n    64→  if (listing) {\n    65→    createNotification(\n    66→      listing.author_id,\n    67→      listingId,\n    68→      \"new_member\",\n    69→      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    70→    );\n    71→  }\n    72→}\n    73→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"67f9a582-414a-4941-8737-ee5f2f8c8808","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:02:12.296Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_notification","task_id":"a8e6194c4a49f53e1","tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","status":"completed","output_file":"","summary":"Deep code review of bookmate","usage":{"total_tokens":74398,"tool_uses":44,"duration_ms":123023},"uuid":"04976464-3c33-480e-a161-b64f6b3f9449","session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SJFzWJw4Xd2BwmRVQgk6HC","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a comprehensive view of the codebase. Let me document all the findings.\n\n---\n\nReviewing the Bookmate Next.js app. Here are the high-confidence findings grouped by severity.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Email duplicate-check query uses raw (non-normalized) email — registration bypass\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts`, line 67\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\nThe duplicate-email check queries the database with the raw value from the request body, before the email is normalized to lowercase. The INSERT on line 81 uses `email.toLowerCase().trim()`. Because the users table stores email in lowercase (from the INSERT), a case-variant registration attempt (e.g., `User@Example.com` when `user@example.com` already exists) will:\n\n1. Pass the duplicate check — the SELECT finds no match because the stored value is lowercase.\n2. Hit the SQLite UNIQUE constraint on `users.email` and return a 500 \"Internal server error\" instead of the friendly 409 conflict response.\n\nThe fix is to normalize before the duplicate check:\n\n```ts\n// line 66 — change:\n.get(email);\n// to:\n.get(email.toLowerCase().trim());\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. `hasApplied` / `applicationStatus` only populated when `requires_approval` is truthy — stale application state visible after the flag is toggled off\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 62–72\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nThe application-status block is guarded by `listing.requires_approval`:\n\n```ts\nif (session.userId && !isMember && listing.requires_approval) {\n```\n\nIf an author creates a listing with `requires_approval = true`, a user applies and is left pending, and the author later edits the listing to turn approval off, the pending application record stays in the DB. On the next GET for that listing, `hasApplied` will be `false` and `applicationStatus` will be `\"\"` even though the user has a pending application row — they can now re-apply via `POST /apply` (which checks `existingApp.status === 'pending'` and correctly blocks) but the UI will show a \"Join\" button rather than the pending state, so the user sees inconsistent UX and could attempt to both join directly and have a pending application simultaneously. The join endpoint does not check for an existing application, so a user can direct-join while they also have a pending application.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 3. `notifyListingAuthor` notifies the author even when the author is the joiner\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 84–88 and `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 54–72\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n`join/route.ts` correctly guards against the author re-joining (line 30), so the author can never trigger `notifyListingAuthor` from this route. However, `notifyListingAuthor` itself has no guard against `author_id === joiner`. The same function is called from other code paths (e.g., Telegram webhook integration). If it is ever called when the joiner is the author, the author receives a self-addressed \"Someone joined your reading group\" notification. The notification function should check `listing.author_id !== joinerId` before creating the notification.\n\n---\n\n### 4. `ratings` — listing `is_full` flag reused as \"group completed\" gate, allows rating of active groups\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts`, lines 83–89\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe rating endpoint uses `is_full` as the proxy for a completed group:\n\n```ts\nif (!listing.is_full) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Ratings are only available for completed reading groups\" }, ...);\n}\n```\n\n`is_full` is set to 1 the moment the group reaches capacity — not after reading is finished. A user who joins as the last member can immediately rate other members before any reading has happened. The client-side `ListingDetail.tsx` shows the rating UI on `listing.is_full && listing.isMember`, compounding this. This is a semantic issue rather than a security vulnerability, but it contradicts the stated intent (\"completed reading groups\").\n\n---\n\n### 5. `GET /api/listings/[id]` leaks `telegram_link` and `discord_link` to unauthenticated users of non-full listings\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 88–102\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe route returns `listing` via the spread `...listing`, which includes `telegram_link` and `discord_link`. For a listing that is not yet full (the public browsing case), those fields are typically empty, but once a link is set the listing cannot be edited or deleted anyway. The concern is that for listings that are full: the guard on line 52 only applies to full listings, returning a 404 to non-members. For non-full listings, any unauthenticated visitor receives the entire listing row including any Telegram/Discord links already stored. This is low-risk today (links are empty unless `is_full` would have already blocked access) but is a latent data-exposure issue if link-setting logic is ever extended to non-full listings.\n\n---\n\n### 6. `GET /api/listings` — `startDate` filter and the default date filter can double-apply conflicting conditions\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 22–45\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\nWhen `includePast` is false (the default) and a `start_date_from` query parameter is provided, both conditions are pushed into the WHERE clause independently:\n\n```ts\nconditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");   // today\n...\nconditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");   // startDateFrom\n```\n\nIf `startDateFrom` is a date before today, the more restrictive \"today\" condition still applies and the parameter silently has no additional effect, which will confuse API consumers. If `startDateFrom` is after today, both conditions are redundant but correct. The more significant issue is there is no way via this API to ask \"show me listings starting from a past date\" because `includePast=true` disables the today floor and `start_date_from` then works correctly — but combining `includePast=false` and a past `start_date_from` yields the union of two conditions that silently ignores the caller's intent. This is a correctness/reliability issue for any client using the filter.\n\n---\n\n### 7. In-memory rate limiter state is lost on process restart; `setInterval` leaks across Next.js hot reloads in dev\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/rate-limit.ts`, lines 15–23\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\nThe `setInterval` at module top level runs the cleanup job on every import in the Next.js dev server, creating multiple concurrent intervals per hot-reload cycle. In production with PM2 this is a restart-clears-state issue (the window resets on every deployment, removing all recorded attempts). Neither of these is a showstopper but the hot-reload interval accumulation can cause excessive memory use during development. The standard mitigation is to guard with `if (!(global as any).__rateLimitCleanup)` before calling `setInterval`.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | File | Line(s) | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|---------|-------|\n| 1 | Critical | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts` | 67 | Duplicate-email check uses raw email, not normalized; case-variant registration returns 500 instead of 409 |\n| 2 | Critical | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` | 62–72 | Application status not returned when `requires_approval` is off; user can direct-join while having a pending application |\n| 3 | Important | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts` | 64–71 | `notifyListingAuthor` lacks author === joiner guard |\n| 4 | Important | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts` | 83–89 | `is_full` used as \"completed\" proxy; enables rating immediately upon group fill |\n| 5 | Important | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` | 88–102 | Full listing row including link fields returned to unauthenticated users on non-full listings |\n| 6 | Important | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts` | 22–45 | Double `start_date >=` conditions silently conflict when `start_date_from` is before today |\n| 7 | Important | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/rate-limit.ts` | 15–23 | `setInterval` leaks on hot reload; rate limit state lost on process restart |"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a8e6194c4a49f53e1 (use SendMessage with to: 'a8e6194c4a49f53e1' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 74787\ntool_uses: 44\nduration_ms: 123025</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"d6c0e44d-6243-41b3-962a-82ef42f3a203","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:03:00.343Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the Bookmate Next.js app at /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs, security issues, logic errors, and code quality problems. \n\nFocus on:\n1. API routes in app/app/api/ - check for auth bypass, SQL injection, missing validation\n2. Client components - check for state bugs, missing error handling, UX issues\n3. Database queries in app/lib/db.ts - check for correctness, missing indexes, race conditions\n4. Session/auth in app/lib/session.ts - check for security issues\n5. Any pages that might have rendering issues or broken functionality\n\nThis is a mature codebase that has already had 3 rounds of code review. Look for subtle bugs or issues that might have been missed. Report only high-confidence findings with file paths and line numbers.","agentId":"a8e6194c4a49f53e1","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have a comprehensive view of the codebase. Let me document all the findings.\n\n---\n\nReviewing the Bookmate Next.js app. Here are the high-confidence findings grouped by severity.\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Email duplicate-check query uses raw (non-normalized) email — registration bypass\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts`, line 67\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\nThe duplicate-email check queries the database with the raw value from the request body, before the email is normalized to lowercase. The INSERT on line 81 uses `email.toLowerCase().trim()`. Because the users table stores email in lowercase (from the INSERT), a case-variant registration attempt (e.g., `User@Example.com` when `user@example.com` already exists) will:\n\n1. Pass the duplicate check — the SELECT finds no match because the stored value is lowercase.\n2. Hit the SQLite UNIQUE constraint on `users.email` and return a 500 \"Internal server error\" instead of the friendly 409 conflict response.\n\nThe fix is to normalize before the duplicate check:\n\n```ts\n// line 66 — change:\n.get(email);\n// to:\n.get(email.toLowerCase().trim());\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. `hasApplied` / `applicationStatus` only populated when `requires_approval` is truthy — stale application state visible after the flag is toggled off\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 62–72\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nThe application-status block is guarded by `listing.requires_approval`:\n\n```ts\nif (session.userId && !isMember && listing.requires_approval) {\n```\n\nIf an author creates a listing with `requires_approval = true`, a user applies and is left pending, and the author later edits the listing to turn approval off, the pending application record stays in the DB. On the next GET for that listing, `hasApplied` will be `false` and `applicationStatus` will be `\"\"` even though the user has a pending application row — they can now re-apply via `POST /apply` (which checks `existingApp.status === 'pending'` and correctly blocks) but the UI will show a \"Join\" button rather than the pending state, so the user sees inconsistent UX and could attempt to both join directly and have a pending application simultaneously. The join endpoint does not check for an existing application, so a user can direct-join while they also have a pending application.\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 3. `notifyListingAuthor` notifies the author even when the author is the joiner\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, lines 84–88 and `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`, lines 54–72\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n`join/route.ts` correctly guards against the author re-joining (line 30), so the author can never trigger `notifyListingAuthor` from this route. However, `notifyListingAuthor` itself has no guard against `author_id === joiner`. The same function is called from other code paths (e.g., Telegram webhook integration). If it is ever called when the joiner is the author, the author receives a self-addressed \"Someone joined your reading group\" notification. The notification function should check `listing.author_id !== joinerId` before creating the notification.\n\n---\n\n### 4. `ratings` — listing `is_full` flag reused as \"group completed\" gate, allows rating of active groups\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts`, lines 83–89\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe rating endpoint uses `is_full` as the proxy for a completed group:\n\n```ts\nif (!listing.is_full) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Ratings are only available for completed reading groups\" }, ...);\n}\n```\n\n`is_full` is set to 1 the moment the group reaches capacity — not after reading is finished. A user who joins as the last member can immediately rate other members before any reading has happened. The client-side `ListingDetail.tsx` shows the rating UI on `listing.is_full && listing.isMember`, compounding this. This is a semantic issue rather than a security vulnerability, but it contradicts the stated intent (\"completed reading groups\").\n\n---\n\n### 5. `GET /api/listings/[id]` leaks `telegram_link` and `discord_link` to unauthenticated users of non-full listings\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`, lines 88–102\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\nThe route returns `listing` via the spread `...listing`, which includes `telegram_link` and `discord_link`. For a listing that is not yet full (the public browsing case), those fields are typically empty, but once a link is set the listing cannot be edited or deleted anyway. The concern is that for listings that are full: the guard on line 52 only applies to full listings, returning a 404 to non-members. For non-full listings, any unauthenticated visitor receives the entire listing row including any Telegram/Discord links already stored. This is low-risk today (links are empty unless `is_full` would have already blocked access) but is a latent data-exposure issue if link-setting logic is ever extended to non-full listings.\n\n---\n\n### 6. `GET /api/listings` — `startDate` filter and the default date filter can double-apply conflicting conditions\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 22–45\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\nWhen `includePast` is false (the default) and a `start_date_from` query parameter is provided, both conditions are pushed into the WHERE clause independently:\n\n```ts\nconditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");   // today\n...\nconditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");   // startDateFrom\n```\n\nIf `startDateFrom` is a date before today, the more restrictive \"today\" condition still applies and the parameter silently has no additional effect, which will confuse API consumers. If `startDateFrom` is after today, both conditions are redundant but correct. The more significant issue is there is no way via this API to ask \"show me listings starting from a past date\" because `includePast=true` disables the today floor and `start_date_from` then works correctly — but combining `includePast=false` and a past `start_date_from` yields the union of two conditions that silently ignores the caller's intent. This is a correctness/reliability issue for any client using the filter.\n\n---\n\n### 7. In-memory rate limiter state is lost on process restart; `setInterval` leaks across Next.js hot reloads in dev\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/rate-limit.ts`, lines 15–23\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\nThe `setInterval` at module top level runs the cleanup job on every import in the Next.js dev server, creating multiple concurrent intervals per hot-reload cycle. In production with PM2 this is a restart-clears-state issue (the window resets on every deployment, removing all recorded attempts). Neither of these is a showstopper but the hot-reload interval accumulation can cause excessive memory use during development. The standard mitigation is to guard with `if (!(global as any).__rateLimitCleanup)` before calling `setInterval`.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | File | Line(s) | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|---------|-------|\n| 1 | Critical | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts` | 67 | Duplicate-email check uses raw email, not normalized; case-variant registration returns 500 instead of 409 |\n| 2 | Critical | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` | 62–72 | Application status not returned when `requires_approval` is off; user can direct-join while having a pending application |\n| 3 | Important | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts` | 64–71 | `notifyListingAuthor` lacks author === joiner guard |\n| 4 | Important | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/ratings/route.ts` | 83–89 | `is_full` used as \"completed\" proxy; enables rating immediately upon group fill |\n| 5 | Important | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` | 88–102 | Full listing row including link fields returned to unauthenticated users on non-full listings |\n| 6 | Important | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts` | 22–45 | Double `start_date >=` conditions silently conflict when `start_date_from` is before today |\n| 7 | Important | `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/rate-limit.ts` | 15–23 | `setInterval` leaks on hot reload; rate limit state lost on process restart |"}],"totalDurationMs":123025,"totalTokens":74787,"totalToolUseCount":44,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":482,"cache_read_input_tokens":72079,"output_tokens":2225,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":482},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UEBm6PSW99egskx9c4UTYS","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\n     3→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     4→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     5→import { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n     6→\n     7→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     8→  try {\n     9→    const { email, password, displayName } = await req.json();\n    10→\n    11→    if (!email || !password || !displayName) {\n    12→      return NextResponse.json(\n    13→        { error: \"Email, password, and display name are required\" },\n    14→        { status: 400 }\n    15→      );\n    16→    }\n    17→\n    18→    // Rate limit: 3 registrations per IP per 15 minutes\n    19→    const ip = req.headers.get(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() || \"unknown\";\n    20→    const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(`register:${ip}`, 3);\n    21→    if (!allowed) {\n    22→      return NextResponse.json(\n    23→        { error: \"Too many registration attempts. Please try again later.\" },\n    24→        { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n    25→      );\n    26→    }\n    27→\n    28→    if (password.length < 8) {\n    29→      return NextResponse.json(\n    30→        { error: \"Password must be at least 8 characters\" },\n    31→        { status: 400 }\n    32→      );\n    33→    }\n    34→\n    35→    // bcrypt only uses the first 72 bytes; cap to prevent DoS via slow hashing\n    36→    if (password.length > 72) {\n    37→      return NextResponse.json(\n    38→        { error: \"Password must be at most 72 characters\" },\n    39→        { status: 400 }\n    40→      );\n    41→    }\n    42→\n    43→    if (displayName.length > 100) {\n    44→      return NextResponse.json(\n    45→        { error: \"Display name must be at most 100 characters\" },\n    46→        { status: 400 }\n    47→      );\n    48→    }\n    49→\n    50→    if (email.length > 254) {\n    51→      return NextResponse.json(\n    52→        { error: \"Email address is too long\" },\n    53→        { status: 400 }\n    54→      );\n    55→    }\n    56→\n    57→    if (!/^[^\\s@]+@[^\\s@]+\\.[^\\s@]+$/.test(email.trim())) {\n    58→      return NextResponse.json(\n    59→        { error: \"Please provide a valid email address\" },\n    60→        { status: 400 }\n    61→      );\n    62→    }\n    63→\n    64→    // Check if email is already taken\n    65→    const existing = db\n    66→      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n    67→      .get(email);\n    68→    if (existing) {\n    69→      return NextResponse.json(\n    70→        { error: \"An account with this email already exists\" },\n    71→        { status: 409 }\n    72→      );\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12);\n    76→\n    77→    const result = db\n    78→      .prepare(\n    79→        \"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\"\n    80→      )\n    81→      .run(email.toLowerCase().trim(), passwordHash, displayName.trim());\n    82→\n    83→    // Auto-login after registration\n    84→    const session = await getSession();\n    85→    session.userId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);\n    86→    session.email = email.toLowerCase().trim();\n    87→    session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n    88→    await session.save();\n    89→\n    90→    return NextResponse.json(\n    91→      {\n    92→        user: {\n    93→          id: result.lastInsertRowid,\n    94→          email: email.toLowerCase().trim(),\n    95→          displayName: displayName.trim(),\n    96→        },\n    97→      },\n    98→      { status: 201 }\n    99→    );\n   100→  } catch (error) {\n   101→    console.error(\"Registration error:\", error);\n   102→    return NextResponse.json(\n   103→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n   104→      { status: 500 }\n   105→    );\n   106→  }\n   107→}\n   108→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"cf4d72dd-e3b6-4d25-802f-7c14a2c83175","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:03:10.845Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  try {\n    const { email, password, displayName } = await req.json();\n\n    if (!email || !password || !displayName) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Email, password, and display name are required\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Rate limit: 3 registrations per IP per 15 minutes\n    const ip = req.headers.get(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() || \"unknown\";\n    const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(`register:${ip}`, 3);\n    if (!allowed) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Too many registration attempts. Please try again later.\" },\n        { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (password.length < 8) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Password must be at least 8 characters\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // bcrypt only uses the first 72 bytes; cap to prevent DoS via slow hashing\n    if (password.length > 72) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Password must be at most 72 characters\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (displayName.length > 100) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Display name must be at most 100 characters\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (email.length > 254) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Email address is too long\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (!/^[^\\s@]+@[^\\s@]+\\.[^\\s@]+$/.test(email.trim())) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Please provide a valid email address\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Check if email is already taken\n    const existing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n      .get(email);\n    if (existing) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"An account with this email already exists\" },\n        { status: 409 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12);\n\n    const result = db\n      .prepare(\n        \"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\"\n      )\n      .run(email.toLowerCase().trim(), passwordHash, displayName.trim());\n\n    // Auto-login after registration\n    const session = await getSession();\n    session.userId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);\n    session.email = email.toLowerCase().trim();\n    session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n    await session.save();\n\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      {\n        user: {\n          id: result.lastInsertRowid,\n          email: email.toLowerCase().trim(),\n          displayName: displayName.trim(),\n        },\n      },\n      { status: 201 }\n    );\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Registration error:\", error);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}\n","numLines":108,"startLine":1,"totalLines":108}}}

 14→  type: string,\n    15→  message: string\n    16→) {\n    17→  db.prepare(\n    18→    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    19→ \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n    51→  }\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→export function notifyListingAuthor(\n    55→  listingId: number,\n    56→  joinerName: string\n    57→) {\n    58→  const listing = db\n    59→    .prepare(\n    60→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    61→    )\n    62→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    63→\n    64→  if (listing) {\n    65→    createNotification(\n    66→      listing.author_id,\n    67→      listingId,\n    68→      \"new_member\",\n    69→      `${joinerName} joined string\n    77→) {\n    78→  const listing = db\n    79→    .prepare(\n    80→      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    81→    )\n    82→    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n    83→\n    84→  if (listing) {\n    85→    createNotification(\n    86→      listing.author_id,\n    87→      listingId,\n    88→      \"application_received\",\n    89→      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    90→    );\n    91→  }\n    92→}\n    93→\n    94→export function notifyApplicationDecision(\n    95→  userId: number,\n    96→  listingId: number,\n    97→  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n    98→) {\n    99→  const listing = db\n   100→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   101→    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n   102→\n   103→  if (listing) {\n   104→    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n   105→    const message =\n   106→      decision === \"approved\"\n   107→        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n   108→        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n   109→    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n   110→  }\n   111→}\n   112→\n   113→export function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n   114→  const listing = db\n   115→    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   116→ 119→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   120→\n   121→  if (listing) {\n   122→    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n   123→    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n   124→      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n   125→      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n   126→    const message = botConfigured\n   127→      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n   128→      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n   129→\n   130→    for (const member of members) {\n   131→      createNotification(\n   132→        member.user_id,\n   133→        listingId,\n   134→        \"group_full\",\n   135→        message\n   136→      );\n   137→    }\n   138→  }\n   139→}\n   140→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"c551fbd6-fab4-460c-b788-1dd2febc910d","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:03:12.371Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts","content":"import db from \"./db\";\nimport { sendEmail } from \"./email\";\n\nfunction getUserEmail(userId: number): string | null {\n  const user = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT email FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(userId) as { email: string } | undefined;\n  return user?.email || null;\n}\n\nexport function createNotification(\n  userId: number,\n  listingId: number | null,\n  type: string,\n  message: string\n) {\n  db.prepare(\n    \"INSERT INTO notifications (user_id, listing_id, type, message) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n  ).run(userId, listingId, type, message);\n\n  // Also send email notification (fire-and-forget)\n  const email = getUserEmail(userId);\n  if (email) {\n    const subject = getEmailSubject(type);\n    sendEmail(email, subject, message).catch(() => {\n      // Email sending is best-effort — don't block on failure\n    });\n  }\n}\n\nfunction getEmailSubject(type: string): string {\n  switch (type) {\n    case \"new_member\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Someone joined your reading group!\";\n    case \"group_full\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your reading group is full!\";\n    case \"application_received\":\n      return \"Bookmate: New application for your reading group\";\n    case \"application_approved\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Your application was approved!\";\n    case \"application_rejected\":\n      return \"Bookmate: Application update\";\n    case \"rating_received\":\n      return \"Bookmate: You received a new rating\";\n    case \"listing_deleted\":\n      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been cancelled\";\n    case \"listing_updated\":\n      return \"Bookmate: A reading group has been updated\";\n    default:\n      return \"Bookmate: Notification\";\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyListingAuthor(\n  listingId: number,\n  joinerName: string\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    createNotification(\n      listing.author_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"new_member\",\n      `${joinerName} joined your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyApplicationReceived(\n  listingId: number,\n  applicantName: string\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT l.author_id, l.book_title FROM listings l WHERE l.id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    createNotification(\n      listing.author_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"application_received\",\n      `${applicantName} applied to join your reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\"`\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyApplicationDecision(\n  userId: number,\n  listingId: number,\n  decision: \"approved\" | \"rejected\"\n) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string } | undefined;\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const type = decision === \"approved\" ? \"application_approved\" : \"application_rejected\";\n    const message =\n      decision === \"approved\"\n        ? `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was approved!`\n        : `Your application to join \"${listing.book_title}\" was not accepted.`;\n    createNotification(userId, listingId, type, message);\n  }\n}\n\nexport function notifyGroupFull(listingId: number) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT book_title, platform_preference FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as { book_title: string; platform_preference: string } | undefined;\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  if (listing) {\n    const platform = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\" ? \"Discord\" : \"Telegram\";\n    const botConfigured = listing.platform_preference === \"discord\"\n      ? !!process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN\n      : !!process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;\n    const message = botConfigured\n      ? `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The ${platform} group will be set up automatically.`\n      : `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" is now full! The organizer will share a ${platform} link soon.`;\n\n    for (const member of members) {\n      createNotification(\n        member.user_id,\n        listingId,\n        \"group_full\",\n        message\n      );\n    }\n  }\n}\n","numLines":140,"startLine":1,"totalLines":140}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015AjKKphydpPJdmrHmFyRAC","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Simple in-memory rate limiter for auth endpoints.\n     3→ * Uses a sliding window approach keyed by identifier (IP + route).\n     4→ * Not shared across cluster workers — acceptable for SQLite single-process setup.\n     5→ */\n     6→\n     7→interface RateLimitEntry {\n     8→  timestamps: number[];\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→const store = new Map<string, RateLimitEntry>();\n    12→\n    13→// Clean up expired entries every 5 minutes\n    14→const CLEANUP_INTERVAL = 5 * 60 * 1000;\n    15→setInterval(() => {\n    16→  const now = Date.now();\n    17→  for (const [key, entry] of store) {\n    18→    entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < 15 * 60 * 1000);\n    19→    if (entry.timestamps.length === 0) {\n    20→      store.delete(key);\n    21→    }\n    22→  }\n    23→}, CLEANUP_INTERVAL);\n    24→\n    25→/**\n    26→ * Check if a request should be rate limited.\n    27→ * @param key - Unique identifier (e.g., IP + email for login, IP for register)\n    28→ * @param maxAttempts - Maximum attempts allowed in the window\n    29→ * @param windowMs - Time window in milliseconds (default: 15 minutes)\n    30→ * @returns Object with allowed boolean and retryAfter seconds (0 if allowed)\n    31→ */\n    32→export function checkRateLimit(\n    33→  key: string,\n    34→  maxAttempts: number = 5,\n    35→  windowMs: number = 15 * 60 * 1000\n    36→): { allowed: boolean; retryAfter: number } {\n    37→  const now = Date.now();\n    38→  let entry = store.get(key);\n    39→\n    40→  if (!entry) {\n    41→    entry = { timestamps: [] };\n    42→    store.set(key, entry);\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Remove expired timestamps\n    46→  entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < windowMs);\n    47→\n    48→  if (entry.timestamps.length >= maxAttempts) {\n    49→    const oldestInWindow = entry.timestamps[0];\n    50→    const retryAfter = Math.ceil((oldestInWindow + windowMs - now) / 1000);\n    51→    return { allowed: false, retryAfter };\n    52→  }\n    53→\n    54→  entry.timestamps.push(now);\n    55→  return { allowed: true, retryAfter: 0 };\n    56→}\n    57→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"6d2b4354-5e83-4944-83fc-c77a730c3ade","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:03:13.465Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/rate-limit.ts","content":"/**\n * Simple in-memory rate limiter for auth endpoints.\n * Uses a sliding window approach keyed by identifier (IP + route).\n * Not shared across cluster workers — acceptable for SQLite single-process setup.\n */\n\ninterface RateLimitEntry {\n  timestamps: number[];\n}\n\nconst store = new Map<string, RateLimitEntry>();\n\n// Clean up expired entries every 5 minutes\nconst CLEANUP_INTERVAL = 5 * 60 * 1000;\nsetInterval(() => {\n  const now = Date.now();\n  for (const [key, entry] of store) {\n    entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < 15 * 60 * 1000);\n    if (entry.timestamps.length === 0) {\n      store.delete(key);\n    }\n  }\n}, CLEANUP_INTERVAL);\n\n/**\n * Check if a request should be rate limited.\n * @param key - Unique identifier (e.g., IP + email for login, IP for register)\n * @param maxAttempts - Maximum attempts allowed in the window\n * @param windowMs - Time window in milliseconds (default: 15 minutes)\n * @returns Object with allowed boolean and retryAfter seconds (0 if allowed)\n */\nexport function checkRateLimit(\n  key: string,\n  maxAttempts: number = 5,\n  windowMs: number = 15 * 60 * 1000\n): { allowed: boolean; retryAfter: number } {\n  const now = Date.now();\n  let entry = store.get(key);\n\n  if (!entry) {\n    entry = { timestamps: [] };\n    store.set(key, entry);\n  }\n\n  // Remove expired timestamps\n  entry.timestamps = entry.timestamps.filter((t) => now - t < windowMs);\n\n  if (entry.timestamps.length >= maxAttempts) {\n    const oldestInWindow = entry.timestamps[0];\n    const retryAfter = Math.ceil((oldestInWindow + windowMs - now) / 1000);\n    return { allowed: false, retryAfter };\n  }\n\n  entry.timestamps.push(now);\n  return { allowed: true, retryAfter: 0 };\n}\n","numLines":57,"startLine":1,"totalLines":57}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HExMGvxhUBNPg7eNwrZmFf","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n    30→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    42→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Check if already a member\n    46→  const existing = db\n    47→    .prepare(\n    48→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    )\n    50→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    51→\n    52→  if (existing) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    57→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    58→    const { count } = db\n    59→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    60→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    61→\n    62→    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    63→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    64→    }\n    65→\n    66→    db.prepare(\n    67→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    68→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    69→\n    70→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    71→    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    72→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    73→    }\n    74→\n    75→    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n    76→  });\n    77→\n    78→  const result = joinTransaction();\n    79→\n    80→  if (result.error) {\n    81→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n    82→  }\n    83→\n    84→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n    85→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n    86→\n    87→  if (result.count === (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    88→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n    89→  }\n    90→\n    91→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n    92→}\n    93→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"a4193704-7eae-4f84-b840-6ada89ab50d7","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:03:15.350Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\nexport async function POST(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Check if already a member\n  const existing = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n\n  if (existing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    const { count } = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n\n    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    }\n\n    db.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n\n    const newCount = count + 1;\n    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    }\n\n    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n  });\n\n  const result = joinTransaction();\n\n  if (result.error) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n\n  if (result.count === (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n}\n","numLines":93,"startLine":1,"totalLines":93}}}

\"@/lib/notifications\";\nimport { isBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\nimport { isBotConfigured as isDiscordBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n\nexport async function GET(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        l.*,\n        u.display_name as author_name\n      FROM listings l\n      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    )\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not u.id\n       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    )\n    .all(listingId) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n\n  const session = await getSession();\n  const isMember = session.userId\n    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    : false;\n  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    : false;\n\n  // PRD: full listings should be invisible to non-participants\n  if (listing.is_full && !isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"This listing is no longer available\" },\n      { status: 404 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Check if the current user has a pending application\n  let hasApplied = false;\n  let applicationStatus = \"\";\n AND user_id = ?\"\n      )\n      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { status: string } | undefined;\n    if (app) {\n      hasApplied = app.status === \"pending\";\n      applicationStatus = app.status;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fetch pending applicants if the viewer is the author\n  let pendingApplicants: { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[] = [];\n  if (isAuthor && listing.requires_approval) {\n    pendingApplicants = db\n      .prepare(\n        `SELECT la.id as application_id, u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, la.applied_at\n         FROM listing_applications la\n         JOIN users u ON la.user_id = u.id\n         WHERE la.listing_id = ? AND la.status = 'pending'\n         ORDER BY la.applied_at ASC`\n      )\n      .all(listingId) as { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[];\n  }\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    listing: {\n      ...listing,\n      members,\n      memberCount: members.length,\n      isMember,\n      isAuthor,\n      hasApplied,\n      applicationStatus,\n      pendingApplicants,\n      currentUserId: session.userId || null,\n      telegramBotConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n      discordBotConfigured: isDiscordBotConfigured(),\n    },\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function PATCH(\n  req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can edit this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n  }\n\n  // Cannot edit if a platform link has been shared (group already formed)\n  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Cannot edit a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const body = await req.json();\n    const {\n      language,\n      readingPace,\n      startDate,\n      meetingFormat,\n      maxGroupSize,\n      requiresApproval,\n      platformPreference,\n    } = body;\n\n    // Validate fields that are provided\n    if (readingPace !== undefined && (!readingPace || readingPace.length > 200)) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Reading pace is required (max 200 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n\n    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n      if (!/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" }, { status: 400 });\n      }\n      const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n      if (startDate < today) {\n        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Start date cannot be in the past\" }, { status: 400 });\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (meetingFormat !== undefined && ![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid meeting format\" }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n\n    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n      const size = parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10);\n      if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" }, { status: 400 });\n      }\n\n      // Cannot reduce below current member count\n      const memberCount = (db\n        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n        .get(listingId) as { count: number }).count;\n\n      if (size < memberCount) {\n        return NextResponse.json(\n          { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${memberCount})` },\n          { status: 400 }\n        );\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (language !== undefined && (!language || typeof language !== \"string\" || language.length > 100)) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Language is required (max 100 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n\n    if (platformPreference !== undefined && ![\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid platform preference\" }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n\n    // Build dynamic UPDATE\n    const updates: string[] = [];\n    const values: unknown[] = [];\n\n    if (language !== undefined) {\n      updates.push(\"language = ?\");\n      values.push(language);\n    }\n    if (readingPace !== undefined) {\n      updates.push(\"reading_pace = ?\");\n      values.push(readingPace.trim());\n    }\n    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n      updates.push(\"start_date = ?\");\n      values.push(startDate);\n    }\n    if (meetingFormat !== undefined) {\n      updates.push(\"meeting_format = ?\");\n      values.push(meetingFormat);\n    }\n    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n      updates.push(\"max_group_size = ?\");\n      values.push(parseInt(maxGroupSize, 10));\n    }\n    if (requiresApproval !== undefined) {\n      updates.push(\"requires_approval = ?\");\n      values.push(requiresApproval ? 1 : 0);\n    }\n    if (platformPreference !== undefined) {\n      updates.push(\"platform_preference = ?\");\n      values.push(platformPreference);\n    }\n\n    if (updates.length === 0) {\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"No fields to update\" }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n\n    values.push(listingId);\n    db.prepare(`UPDATE listings SET ${updates.join(\", \")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...values);\n\n    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Update listing error:\", error);\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function DELETE(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can delete this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n  }\n\n  // Cannot delete if a platform link has been shared\n  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Cannot delete a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  try {\n    // Get members to notify (excluding the author)\n    const members = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != ?\")\n      .all(listingId, session.userId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n    // Delete in a transaction (notifications sent after, so they aren't wiped)\n    const deleteTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM notifications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM ratings WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    });\n\n    deleteTransaction();\n\n    // Send deletion notifications after transaction (listing_id is NULL since listing is gone)\n    for (const member of members) {\n      try {\n        createNotification(\n          member.user_id,\n          null,\n          \"listing_deleted\",\n          `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" has been cancelled by the organizer.`\n        );\n      } catch {\n        // Notification failure should not block response\n      }\n    }\n\n    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Delete listing error:\", error);\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n  }\n}\n","numLines":317,"startLine":1,"totalLines":317}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01NchefW6ZK8FMzjN47nawZB","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Good, I've read all the files. Now let me fix the confirmed bugs. I'll focus on the critical and important issues:\n\n1. **Critical**: Email duplicate check not normalized (causes 500 instead of 409)\n2. **Important**: Application status not checked when `requires_approval` is off + join doesn't check for pending apps\n3. **Important**: Rate limiter `setInterval` leaks on hot reload\n4. **Important**: Listing response leaks telegram/discord links to non-members\n\nLet me also check the listings route for the date filter issue:"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3680,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5819,"cache_read_input_tokens":33678,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":5819},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"a49b56e7-a7aa-43b3-a9bb-8d9e8bd0e975"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NtRPj4632TS1r4xu5VbyGT","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→\n     4→const PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n     5→\n     6→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n     7→  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n     8→\n     9→  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n    10→  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n    11→  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n    12→  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n    13→  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n    14→  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n    15→  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n    16→  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n    17→\n    18→  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n    19→  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n    20→\n    21→  // By default, hide listings with start dates in the past\n    22→  if (!includePast) {\n    23→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    24→    params.push(new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0]);\n    25→  }\n    26→\n    27→  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    28→    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    29→    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    33→    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    34→    params.push(meetingFormat);\n    35→  }\n    36→\n    37→  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    38→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    39→    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n    40→  }\n    41→\n    42→  if (startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom)) {\n    43→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    44→    params.push(startDateFrom);\n    45→  }\n    46→\n    47→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    48→\n    49→  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    50→  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n    51→  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n    52→\n    53→  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n    54→\n    55→  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n    56→  const countResult = db\n    57→    .prepare(\n    58→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    59→    )\n    60→    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n    61→\n    62→  const listings = db\n    63→    .prepare(\n    64→      `SELECT\n    65→        l.*,\n    66→        u.display_name as author_name,\n    67→        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    68→      FROM listings l\n    69→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    70→      WHERE ${whereClause}\n    71→      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n    72→      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    73→    )\n    74→    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n    75→\n    76→  return NextResponse.json({\n    77→    listings,\n    78→    pagination: {\n    79→      page,\n    80→      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n    81→      total: countResult.total,\n    82→      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    83→    },\n    84→  });\n    85→}\n    86→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"c6180419-4a19-447b-bafe-dc056aecdc4f","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:03:49.973Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n\nexport async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n\n  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n\n  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n\n  // By default, hide listings with start dates in the past\n  if (!includePast) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0]);\n  }\n\n  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n  }\n\n  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    params.push(meetingFormat);\n  }\n\n  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n  }\n\n  if (startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom)) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(startDateFrom);\n  }\n\n  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n\n  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n\n  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n\n  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n  const countResult = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    )\n    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n\n  const listings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        l.*,\n        u.display_name as author_name,\n        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n      FROM listings l\n      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n      WHERE ${whereClause}\n      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    )\n    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    listings,\n    pagination: {\n      page,\n      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n      total: countResult.total,\n      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    },\n  });\n}\n","numLines":86,"startLine":1,"totalLines":86}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0143tV8iqJVeR923un4aW4mZ","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"661d5038-d930-4e21-8f3a-b6e8597abfb8","uuid":"09155c4e-7178-4529-86dd-32d62aa8fe2f","timestamp":"2026-03-23T20:07:38.273Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts","oldString":"    // Check if email is already taken\n    const existing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n      .get(email);\n    if (existing) {","newString":"    // Normalize email before duplicate check (INSERT stores lowercase)\n    const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase().trim();\n\n    // Check if email is already taken\n    const existing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n      .get(normalizedEmail);\n    if (existing) {","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  try {\n    const { email, password, displayName } = await req.json();\n\n    if (!email || !password || !displayName) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Email, password, and display name are required\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Rate limit: 3 registrations per IP per 15 minutes\n    const ip = req.headers.get(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() || \"unknown\";\n    const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(`register:${ip}`, 3);\n    if (!allowed) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Too many registration attempts. Please try again later.\" },\n        { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (password.length < 8) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Password must be at least 8 characters\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // bcrypt only uses the first 72 bytes; cap to prevent DoS via slow hashing\n    if (password.length > 72) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Password must be at most 72 characters\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (displayName.length > 100) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Display name must be at most 100 characters\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (email.length > 254) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Email address is too long\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (!/^[^\\s@]+@[^\\s@]+\\.[^\\s@]+$/.test(email.trim())) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Please provide a valid email address\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Check if email is already taken\n    const existing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n      .get(email);\n    if (existing) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"An account with this email already exists\" },\n        { status: 409 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12);\n\n    const result = db\n      .prepare(\n        \"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\"\n      )\n      .run(email.toLowerCase().trim(), passwordHash, displayName.trim());\n\n    // Auto-login after registration\n    const session = await getSession();\n    session.userId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);\n    session.email = email.toLowerCase().trim();\n    session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n    await session.save();\n\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      {\n        user: {\n          id: result.lastInsertRowid,\n          email: email.toLowerCase().trim(),\n          displayName: displayName.trim(),\n        },\n      },\n      { status: 201 }\n    );\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Registration error:\", error);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":61,"oldLines":10,"newStart":61,"newLines":13,"lines":["       );","     }"," ","+    // Normalize email before duplicate check (INSERT stores lowercase)","+    const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase().trim();","+","     // Check if email is already taken","     const existing = db","       .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")","-      .get(email);","+      .get(normalizedEmail);","     if (existing) {","       return NextResponse.json(","         { error: \"An account with this email already exists\" },"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
